sections.prose: Cleveland-Cliffs Inc. (NYSE: CLF), incorporated in Ohio and classified under NAICS 331110 (iron & steel mills) (10-K p.1), is the largest flat-rolled steel producer in North America, serving automotive and infrastructure end markets.

FY2024 revenue was $19,185M with management-prepared Adjusted EBITDA of $1,200M (10-K p.3). Net debt / EBITDA leverage is ~3.11x and interest coverage (DSCR) ~4.00x; the current ratio is 2.00x, indicating adequate near-term liquidity. Total debt of $3,777M stands against $6,500M of equity (10-K p.2).

The internal model assigns an indicative grade of BB- (conservative, through-the-cycle). The public agency issuer rating sits in the BB category; the one-notch gap reflects a methodology difference, not a data discrepancy.

OFAC/PEP screening returned no matches across the entity, directors, and UBOs. Adverse-media surfaced one low-severity, resolved environmental item. A senior ABL lien on inventory and receivables exists with no conflicting junior liens.

The principal risk is steel-sector cyclicality and automotive-demand sensitivity (10-K p.4). Mitigants include scale as the largest North American flat-rolled producer, diversified end markets, and adequate liquidity. Collateral comprises inventory and receivables pledged under the ABL facility (10-K p.4).

Recommend approval of the facility subject to standard covenants, given adequate coverage (DSCR ~4.00x), moderate leverage (~3.11x), and clean diligence. Monitor cyclical exposure and any agency rating actions.
